_meta.what: Fly-CL reproduction with the AUTHORS' ORIGINAL framework (github.com/gfyddha/Fly-CL), unmodified pipeline, on CIFAR-100 / CUB-200-2011 / VTAB
_meta.hyperparams: exactly scripts/test_{cifar,cub,vtab}.sh: expand_dim=10000, synaptic_degree=300, coding_level=0.3, ridge 1e6..1e10 (GCV), batch 128, seeds cifar=1993 cub/vtab=2023
_meta.code_change: only models/load_model.py got an ADDITIVE branch 'vit_base_patch16_224_in21k' -> timm vit_base_patch16_224.augreg_in21k, matching the checkpoint the authors' pretrained_model/download.sh fetches (augreg IN21k, no IN1k finetune). The default branch (pretrained=True -> augreg2_in21k_ft_in1k) was left untouched and also run for comparison.
_meta.metric: A_bar = Accumulated Accuracy printed by official main.py = mean over stages of A_t

conclusion: With the checkpoint the authors' own download.sh specifies (augreg IN21k), the original framework reproduces the paper to within noise: CIFAR-100 93.88 vs 93.89, CUB 93.84 vs 93.84 (exact), VTAB 95.73 vs 96.54 (-0.81, ~2 sigma). This also resolves the checkpoint ambiguity in the official repo: load_model.py's pretrained=True actually pulls augreg2_in21k_ft_in1k, which scores 1-2 points lower on CUB/VTAB; the paper numbers correspond to the IN21k-only augreg checkpoint.
